title: lycopersicon esculentum subsp. galenii
intro: Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. galenii, also known as the Galapagos tomato, is a subspecies of tomato native to the Galapagos Islands. It is a wild tomato species that has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The Galapagos tomato has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions, including high blood pressure and certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. galenii can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
